title: ProjectConfig
description: Per-project forge configuration. Lives at <project-root>/.forge/project.json. Declares the typed testProcess gate contract (local/ci/acceptance), M2 Studio fields (northStar, instructions, demoProcess, skills, kb, artifactRoot), the release process, and optional logging knobs, metrics, and sweep plug-ins. The authoritative validator is orchestrator/project-config.ts validateProjectConfig; this schema is the operator-facing reference.

testProcess.description: R1-03-F1: the typed test-process contract object — the single JSON source of the project's gate fields, superseding the flat quality_gate_cmd/ci_gate/ci_fix_cmd/ci_gate_unset_env/acceptance_gate keys (now rejected fail-closed with a migration message — no permanent dual-shape shim). Declares the TWO-TIER GATE MODEL: `local` is the fast per-WI gate the developer-loop + unifier run every iteration (contract C1); `ci` is the full CI delivery net run once, before a PR opens, as the final merge tier (C1b); `acceptance` is the optional live-acceptance tier for external-resource projects whose behaviour can only be verified against a live system (C7). Each sub-object accepts an optional `timeoutMs` per ADR-036's precedence: the project-declared value overrides the built-in default, but the operator's per-run FORGE_*_TIMEOUT_MS env var still wins over both. The authoritative types are TestProcess/TestProcessLocal/TestProcessCi/TestProcessAcceptance in orchestrator/project-config.ts.

local.description: The INNER tier of the two-tier gate model (contract C1) — the fast, deterministic, per-WI gate the developer-loop + unifier run between iterations. Must exit 0 on a green tree, be fast (≤ 10s), and be genuinely discriminating (fail before the work exists, pass only when the acceptance criteria are met) — see the C1 failure modes (hollow/false-pass/poisoned) in docs/forge-project-contract.md. `local` (and its `cmd`) is required by the TestProcessLocal type, but the JSON MAY OMIT it: when project.json has no `testProcess.local.cmd`, loadProjectConfig single-sources it from the `.forge/quality_gate_cmd` sidecar (a single whitespace-separated command line) before validation runs — the JSON wins when both are present. A config with neither throws (fail-closed).

cmd.description: Argv-style command. Must be a single command (no `&&`/`;`/`|` chaining) and not a known-slow umbrella (playwright/cypress/e2e/integration); see contract C1 and docs/gate-script-template.md for the committed-gate-script escape hatch when one command can't express the gate.

timeoutMs.description: Overrides the built-in 30-minute default gate timeout. FORGE_GATE_TIMEOUT_MS (per-run operator override) still wins over this declared value.

ci.description: The MERGE tier of the two-tier gate model (contract C1b) — the project's FULL CI verification, mirroring its CI workflow. Run DIRECTLY (operator-trusted, so a `["bash","-c","…&&…"]` chain is permitted here, unlike the pipe-banned per-WI `local` gate) as the final delivery gate before a PR opens; a red `ci` gate routes to triage instead of shipping a CI-red PR. Optional — when populated, C1b requires every WI's `local.cmd` to be the `ci.cmd` or a strict subset subsumed by it.

cmd.description: Argv-style command for the full CI verification, e.g. ["bash","-c","make test && golangci-lint run ./... && make terrafmt-check"].

fixCmd.description: The project's auto-formatters (e.g. make fmt + make terrafmt), applied best-effort BEFORE `cmd` so the PR is fmt-clean. Optional.

unsetEnv.description: Env-var names STRIPPED when running `cmd`/`fixCmd` (contract A3), so the delivery gate is hermetic regardless of the cycle env. Example: ["TF_ACC"] — the serve env sets TF_ACC=1 so per-WI live-acceptance gates run, but the CI gate must run WITHOUT it (else the whole live suite runs, failing on env GitHub never has). `PATH`/`HOME`/`SHELL` are blocklisted — unsetting a process fundamental would weaken the gate child (e.g. PATH loss → wrong-binary resolution) rather than act as a live-test trigger. Optional — absent ⇒ env inherited unchanged.

timeoutMs.description: Overrides the built-in 20-minute default CI-gate timeout. FORGE_CI_GATE_TIMEOUT_MS still wins over this declared value.

acceptance.description: The live-acceptance tier (contract C7). For external-resource projects whose behaviour can only be proven against a live system, `local` stays fast/creds-free/deterministic while `acceptance` requires the PM to emit a mandatory live-acceptance work item. Optional — absent ⇒ no check.

match.description: Substring that identifies a live-acceptance gate in a WI's testProcess.local.cmd (any argv token contains it), e.g. 'acceptancetests'.

required.description: When true, the PM phase HARD-FAILS the cycle unless ≥1 emitted WI has a local.cmd token containing `match` — guaranteeing every initiative is proven by a live acceptance test.

requiresEnv.description: Env vars that MUST be set for a matching (live-acc) gate to actually run live, e.g. ["TF_ACC"]. If a WI's gate matches `match` but one is unset, the dev-loop ERRORS the gate instead of letting a skipped runner (`ok pkg 0.00s`) false-pass. Optional — list every var the test's PreCheck demands.

timeoutMs.description: Reserved: acceptance gates run as per-WI gates, so FORGE_GATE_TIMEOUT_MS semantics apply.

standing_work_item_acs.description: Verbatim acceptance-criterion statements forge appends to EVERY work item the PM emits, as a '## Standing acceptance criteria (project contract)' body section. Encodes project-wide testing invariants (e.g. 'proven by a live acceptance test', 'must not redden CI') as static standing ACs, removing per-WI PM judgment. Optional. See contract A2.

northStar.description: M2 Studio field. One-liner mission statement (≤140 chars) for the project. Flows into the planner's context. Optional.

instructions.description: M2 Studio field. Free-text standing instructions injected into every PM prompt (generalises standing_work_item_acs into prose guidance). Optional.

demoProcess.description: M2 Studio field. Typed demo steps the unifier executes. Must include ≥1 capture step and ≥1 verify step (forge preflight warns if absent). Optional.

kind.description: capture = record real evidence (output/screenshot/API GET); verify = assert the captured evidence against expectations; present = attach the evidence to the PR.

text.description: Human-readable description of the demo step.

skills.description: M2 Studio field. Skill slugs bound to this project (project-action recipes, typically under <project>/forge/skills or .forge/skills). Optional.

kb.description: M2 Studio field. KB id bound to this project, or null to explicitly leave it unbound. Optional (absent ⇒ unset).

artifactRoot.description: A project-root-relative subdirectory (default '.') scoping the only forge artifacts committed in the project repo — the per-cycle in-PR demo (<artifactRoot>/history/<id>/demo) and project-action skills (<artifactRoot>/skills/). Per ADR 035, Brain 3 and durable history are forge-owned and central in the forge repo (brain/projects/<name>/ and _logs/<cycleId>/artifacts/), not governed by artifactRoot. Must be a clean relative path (no leading slash, no '..'). Optional.

logging.description: Optional logging / heartbeat knobs. Tunes forge-side behaviour without changing forge source.

heartbeat_seconds.description: Cadence in seconds for the agent_heartbeat liveness pulse (default 15s). Tune if the project's SDK calls are unusually long-running.

metrics.description: Optional holistic metrics block. Required for any project that wants exploration-shape initiatives; omit otherwise.

command.description: Argv-style command emitting one or more scalar metrics on stdout.

baselines_dir.description: Directory holding locked baseline markdown files.

tolerance_pct.description: Allowable percentage drift before flagging regression.

sweep.description: Optional parameter-sweep plug-in points. Consumed by skills/project-sweep when the unifier runs in harness-runner mode for type: exploration manifests.

start_command.description: Argv-style command that brings the testbed up.

draw_function.description: Path (worktree-relative) to a module exporting a sample-draw function.

measurement_extractor.description: Path (worktree-relative) to a module that parses metrics.command output.

releaseProcess.description: Optional release-process declaration: the repo-side prep a cycle performs before merge. Mirrors demoProcess — typed, optional, fail-closed when malformed. Tagging the release (git tag) and publishing (npm/registry push) are CI's job, NOT forge step kinds. Absent ⇒ no release steps.

steps.description: Ordered release steps forge performs. Each step is tagged with a kind (docs | changelog | version) and a phase (in-cycle | pre-merge).

kind.description: docs = refresh project docs; changelog = write a changelog entry; version = bump the version file. Tag/publish are CI's job, not a forge step kind.

phase.description: in-cycle = during the dev-loop alongside feature work; pre-merge = after the dev-loop, before the unifier opens the PR.

text.description: Human-readable description of the release step.

command.description: Optional argv-style command forge runs to perform the step.

versionFile.description: Worktree-relative path to the file holding the project version (clean relative path — no leading slash, no '..').

changelogPath.description: Worktree-relative path to the changelog file (clean relative path — no leading slash, no '..').

docsDir.description: Worktree-relative directory holding the project's docs (clean relative path — no leading slash, no '..').
